premises: Every mammal has a neocortex and produces milk to feed its young. A completely hairless and scale-covered creature named the Glimmer was recently analyzed by biologists. The biological analysis showed that the Glimmer does not produce milk.

premises: For any prediction P, if P is possibly true, there is at least one accessible world where P is true. Prediction 'Market_Crashes' is possibly true. If a prediction is true in at least one accessible world, it is not necessarily false.
conclusion: Prediction 'Market_Crashes' is necessarily false.
label: False

premises: For any state S, if S is contingently true, then S is true in the actual world but false in at least one possible world. State 'Earth_Has_One_Moon' is contingently true.
conclusion: State 'Earth_Has_One_Moon' is necessarily true.
label: False

premises: For any password P, if it is possible to crack P, P is deemed insecure. If P is deemed insecure, it must be replaced. It is possible to crack Password Admin123.
conclusion: Password Admin123 must be replaced.
label: True

premises: For any geometric truth G, if G is valid in Euclidean geometry, G is necessarily true within that specific framework. Truth 'Parallel_Lines_Never_Meet' is valid in Euclidean geometry.
conclusion: Truth 'Parallel_Lines_Never_Meet' is necessarily true within that specific framework.
label: True

premises: For any fundamental particle P, if P is necessarily massless, P travels exactly at the speed of light. Particle Photon is necessarily massless.
conclusion: Particle Photon travels exactly at the speed of light.
label: True

premises: For any entities X and Y, X is disjoint from Y if and only if X does not overlap Y. Component M overlaps Component N.
conclusion: Component M is disjoint from Component N.
label: False

premises: For any entities X, Y, and Z, if X and Y compose Z, then Z is the fusion of X and Y. If Z is the fusion of X and Y, anything that overlaps Z must overlap either X or Y. Left_Half and Right_Half compose the Shield. Dart_1 overlaps the Shield.
conclusion: Dart_1 overlaps either Left_Half or Right_Half.
label: True

premises: For any physical objects X and Y, if X is a functional component of Y, X is a part of Y. If X is a part of Y, and Y is destroyed, X is not necessarily destroyed. The motherboard is a functional component of the laptop. The laptop is destroyed.
conclusion: The motherboard is necessarily destroyed.
label: False

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is an improper part of Y, then X is a part of Y and X is identical to Y. Sector 7G is an improper part of Sector 7G.
conclusion: Sector 7G is identical to Sector 7G.
label: True

premises: For any time intervals T1 and T2, if T1 is a temporal part of T2, the duration of T1 is strictly less than or equal to the duration of T2. The First_Half is a temporal part of the Match.
conclusion: The duration of the First_Half is strictly less than or equal to the duration of the Match.
label: True

premises: For any software system S, if module M is a dependent part of S, S cannot compile without M. Module Auth is a dependent part of System Backend. Module Auth is missing.
conclusion: System Backend can compile.
label: False

premises: For any mechanical watch W, if gear G is a moving part of W, the cessation of G causes W to stop. Gear Alpha is a moving part of Watch Omega. Gear Alpha ceases.
conclusion: Watch Omega stops.
label: True

premises: For any organism O, if O regenerates, a lost proper part is replaced by a biologically identical new proper part. Organism Starfish regenerates. Organism Starfish loses proper part Arm_Right.
conclusion: Proper part Arm_Right is replaced by a biologically identical new proper part.
label: True

premises: For any logical proof P, if lemma L is a part of P, the truth of P necessitates the truth of L. Lemma 3 is a part of Proof Alpha. Proof Alpha is true.
conclusion: Lemma 3 is true.
label: True

premises: For any universe U, if U allows unrestricted mereological composition, any two arbitrary disjoint objects form a valid fusion object within U. Universe Delta allows unrestricted mereological composition. Object Pen and Object Moon are two arbitrary disjoint objects.
conclusion: Object Pen and Object Moon form a valid fusion object within Universe Delta.
label: True
